zh.Chelsworth, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可傑爾斯沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Robert Abbott於1470年出生在英國賀特福爾時，他的父親William Simon Abbott10歲，母親Alice Abbott18歲。他於1491年與Mary Alice Turman在英國賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1541年住在英國英格蘭素福爾可傑爾斯沃斯。他去世於1532年11月21日在英國賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Robert Abbott于1470年出生在英国賀特福爾时，他的父亲William Simon Abbott10岁，母亲Alice Abbott18岁。他于1491年与Mary Alice Turman在英国賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1541年住在英国英格兰素福爾可傑爾斯沃斯。他去世于1532年11月21日在英国賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Abbott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English abbod, abbot(t), abbat ‘abbot’ (Old English abbod) or Old French abet ‘priest’. Both the Old English and the Old French term are derived from Late Latin abbas (genitive abbatis) ‘priest’, from Greek abbas, from Aramaic aba ‘father’. The nickname was presumably a joking reference to a person's behaviour. In the US, the English name is also sometimes a translation of a cognate or equivalent European name, e.g. Italian Abate , Spanish Abad , or German Abt .

History: George Abbot from Yorkshire, England, settled in Andover, MA, in 1640; he had numerous prominent descendants. George Abbott (probably not the same man) died in Rowley, MA, in 1647. Another early migrant was James Abbott, who came from Somerset, England, to Long Island, NY, in the 17th century.

variants.name: Abate
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Carmelo, Carmine, Gaetano, Vito, Angelo, Canio, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo.

Italian: from abate ‘priest’ (from Late Latin abbas ‘priest’, genitive abbatis). It could be a nickname, perhaps given to someone who behaved in a priest-like manner, an occupational name for the servant of a priest, or it may denote an (illegitimate) son of a priest. Compare Labate .

variants.name: Abbot
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of Abbott . The spelling with one -t is more typically Scottish than English, and in Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland, this is sometimes an Anglicized form of McNabb .

variants.name: Abbotts
meanings: topographic or occupational name from Middle English (atte) abbotes ‘(at the, of the) abbot's (house or quarters)’, a name for someone who lived or worked at such a place, presumably a servant.
variant of Abbott , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When William Robert Abbott was born in 1470, in Hertfordshire, England, his father, William Simon Abbott, was 10 and his mother, Alice Abbott, was 18. He married Mary Alice Turman in 1491, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Chelsworth, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom in 1541. He died on 21 November 1532, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 62.

Il est décédé le 21 novembre 1532, à Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Robert Abbott(이)가 1470년 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Simon Abbott(은)는 10세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Abbott(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1491년 영국 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 Mary Alice Turman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1541년도에 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 첼스워스에서 살았다. 그는 1532년 11월 21일 영국 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Robert Abbott родился в 1470 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Simon Abbott было 10 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Abbott, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Alice Turman в 1491 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности челсворт, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в 1541 году. Он умер 21 ноября 1532 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.
